news.t: 4 Reasons Why Inverse ETFs Are Broken & Here's A Better Solution
The Best Inverse ETFs of the 2020 Bear Market
A Conversation with a Short Seller
Long-short ETFs offer investors a 'smarter way to hedge,' fund manager says as demand rises

holdings.infoBox: HDGE is an equity ETF with a total of 58 individual holdings. The top holdings are Cash stock at 79.69%, Fidelity Instl Govt 657 C at 61.39%, Derivatives Collateral Cf Secured at 51.46%, Blackrock Federal Fd 30 Instl at 3.11%, and Procore Technologies at -0.63%.

overview.description: The AdvisorShares Ranger Equity Bear ETF (HDGE) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in total market equity. The fund is actively-managed fund that attempts to achieve positive returns by shorting US-listed companies believed to have low earnings quality or use aggressive accounting policies. HDGE was launched on Jan 26, 2011 and is issued by AdvisorShares.

data.title: 4 Reasons Why Inverse ETFs Are Broken & Here's A Better Solution
data.text: There is a big problem on Wall Street that almost no one is talking about - Inverse products simply do not work as well as actively managed products.

data.title: The Best Inverse ETFs of the 2020 Bear Market
data.text: The best inverse ETFs of the 2020 bear market were RWM, DOG, and HDGE.

data.title: A Conversation with a Short Seller
data.text: Short seller David Tice, new CIO of AdvisorShares Ranger Equity Bear ETF (HDGE), discusses short opportunities in a post-COVID world.

data.title: Long-short ETFs offer investors a 'smarter way to hedge,' fund manager says as demand rises
data.text: Exchange-traded funds that leverage short-selling strategies can help investors diversify their portfolios and hedge against unexpected declines, says Noah Hamman, CEO and founder of AdvisorShares.

data.title: Long-short ETFs see rising interest — AdvisorShares CEO breaks down the strategy
data.text: Noah Hamman, CEO and founder of AdvisorShares, explains how his firm's long-short exchange-traded funds can help investors diversify their portfolios and hedge against unexpected declines. With CNBC's...
